channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nunavut MP Lori Idlout Leaves NDP to Sit with Liberals, Affecting Parliamentary Balance
item.description: Nunavut Member of Parliament Lori Idlout has officially left the New Democratic Party (NDP) to sit with the Liberal Party, a move that could strengthen Prime Minister Mark Carney's position in the Hou...

item.title: How Rapture Beliefs Influence U.S. Foreign Policy
item.description: This article dey examine how one kind religious belief wey dem dey call premillennial dispensationalism, or Rapture theology, don dey shape American foreign policy for decades. E start with John Nelso...

item.title: Middle East conflict causes surge in airline fuel costs and ticket prices
item.description: Several international airlines have announced significant increases in ticket prices due to a sharp rise in jet fuel costs caused by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Fuel prices have jumped fr...

item.title: Commons approves reforms limiting jury trials for less serious offences
item.description: Legislation aiming to restrict jury trials to the most serious cases has successfully cleared its first significant vote in the House of Commons. The Courts and Tribunals Bill passed by 304 votes to 2...

item.title: Britney Spears’ Booking Photo Remains Private Following DUI Arrest
item.description: Britney Spears was arrested on March 4 in Ventura County, California, for allegedly driving under the influence. She was reportedly pulled over by California Highway Patrol officers for driving errati...

item.title: DHS Reassigns CBP Privacy Officials Over Dispute on FOIA Record Handling
item.description: Earlier this year, the U.S. Department of Homeland Security reassigned several senior Customs and Border Protection (CBP) privacy officials after they opposed directives to mislabel and withhold priva...

item.title: Government to Release ₱5,000 Fuel Subsidy for PUV Drivers Amid Rising Oil Prices
item.description: The Philippine government will start releasing a ₱5,000 fuel subsidy to public utility vehicle (PUV) drivers next week, a move intended to cushion the impact of skyrocketing oil prices triggered by re...

item.title: KSE-100 recovers strongly as investor sentiment improves
item.description: On Wednesday, Pakistan Stock Exchange witnessed a notable recovery with the KSE-100 index rising more than 2,000 points in early trading, reaching 158,382 points. This marked a 1.33 per cent increase ...

item.title: Russia Becomes Leading Gold Seller Amid Economic Pressure
item.description: In January 2026, the Central Bank of Russia became the largest gold seller among central banks, moving 9 tons in a single month. This trend began at the start of the Ukraine war in February 2022, with...

item.title: Aboriginal Communities Face Unequal Support During NT Floods
item.description: Flooding across the Northern Territory's Big Rivers region has highlighted major gaps in emergency support for remote Aboriginal communities. The North Australian Aboriginal Justice Agency (NAAJA) has...

item.title: Alberta Cuts All Provincial Funding to Historical Society
item.description: The Historical Society of Alberta (HSA), founded in 1907 by the province’s first premier Alexander Rutherford, faces a sudden and total loss of provincial funding following Alberta’s 2026 budget annou...

item.title: Balancing affordability and investment for South Africa’s digital networks
item.description: South Africans have long debated the cost of mobile data, with the #DataMustFall movement pushing for lower prices and greater digital inclusion. Following a Competition Commission inquiry in 2019, lo...

item.title: Why the Assembly Speaker should allow the Sakaja impeachment process to proceed lawfully
item.description: The article argues that the Speaker of the Nairobi County Assembly has a duty to allow any properly framed bid to remove Governor Johnson Sakaja from office to be heard and prosecuted, instead of bloc...

item.title: Major News Updates Across Nigeria – March 11, 2026
item.description: Today Naija News bring full gist of the top stories wey dey happen for inside Nigeria. Senate President Godswill Akpabio and other National Assembly leaders go meet President Bola Tinubu for Aso Rock ...

item.title: Examining Labour's asylum policy and its alignment with traditional party values
item.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood has defended her plan to reform the UK’s asylum system by claiming it is grounded in Labour Party values. Her proposals include curbing government support for some asylu...

item.title: Tanzania Prisons prepare for challenging Mainland Premier League clash with Yanga
item.description: Tanzania Prisons FC is set to face defending champions Young Africans S.C. (Yanga) in a Mainland Tanzania Premier League match on Thursday. Newly appointed coach Shadrack Nsajigwa has admitted the tas...

item.title: Alex Eala eliminated from Indian Wells after straight-sets defeat to Linda Nosková
item.description: Filipina tennis star Alex Eala's impressive run at the BNP Paribas Open in Indian Wells came to an end on March 11, 2026, as she suffered a decisive loss to Czech player Linda Nosková in the Round of ...

item.title: ASHA Microfinance Bank honours women entrepreneurs with outreach and new ambassador
item.description: ASHA Microfinance Bank don mark International Women’s Day for Mushin Market with big celebration wey show say dem dey serious about empowering women wey dey run business. Market leaders, traders, and ...

item.title: Anthropic to establish Australian operations and invest in local data infrastructure
item.description: Anthropic, the US-based artificial intelligence company known for its agent coding platform, is officially expanding into Australia and New Zealand. The firm plans to base its regional operations in S...

item.title: Hariharan Discusses the Rise of Independent Music and His Ghazal Journey
item.description: Renowned singer Hariharan, celebrated for his versatility across genres, continues to place ghazals at the heart of his musical journey. His latest album, 'Jaan Meri', features five ghazals with lyric...
